data.title: Yum China names Adrian Ding as CFO
data.description:
• Yum China (NYSE:YUMC) said it has appointed Adrian Ding as its finance chief, effective on March 6.
  • Ding has been serving as acting CFO since October 2024.
  • "Over the last five months, he (Ding) has made significant contributions, proving himself as the ideal candidate to serve as the Chief Financial Officer of Yum China. He has effectively led the finance team, strengthened our engagement with stakeholders, including the capital markets, and played a pivotal role in building Yum China's growth momentum," said Joey Wat, CEO of Yum China.
  • Ding had joined the firm in March 2019 as the vice president of Corporate Finance and served as Chief Investment Officer from February 2020 to March 2025.

data.title: DoorDash rallies after S&P 500 Index inclusion
data.description:
DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) moved higher in early trading on Monday after S&P Dow Jones Indices announced the stock will be added to the S&P 500 Index on March 24. Due to the over $10 trillion indexed or benchmarked to the S&P 500 globally as the world’s most widely followed index, the index inclusion is considered a positive catalyst for DASH shares. 

DoorDash (NASDAQ:DASH) became eligible for S&P inclusion by recording its second quarter of GAAP profitability, and exceeding break-even for the four trailing quarters of GAAP profit.

Bank of America also pointed to positive recent data for DoorDash (DASH) in its recent Gig Data Dive. The firm considers Buy-rated DoorDash (DASH) one of the best-TAM expansion stories in the Internet sector. "We also remain constructive given: 1) NV-driven upside to top-line could be durable for several quarters, with a path to positive NV unit economics; 2) Ad benefit just getting started, earlier stage vs peers; 3) Incremental margin expectations not elevated vs peers," noted analyst Michael McGovern.

Shares of DoorDash (DASH) rallied 4.8% in premarket trading on Monday to $186.74 vs. the 52-week range of $99.32 to $215.25. Short interest on the online food delivery stock stands at 2.4% of the total float.

data.title: Ford plans to inject up to $4.8B into its struggling German subsidiary
data.description:
Ford Motor (NYSE:F) announced on Monday that the company will inject up to 4.4 billion euros ($4.76 billion) into its Ford-Werke German arm as it looks to spark the European business.

"By recapitalising our German operations, we are supporting the transformation of our business in Europe and strengthening our ability to compete with a fresh product portfolio," stated Ford (NYSE:F) Vice Chairman John Lawler. "To build a sustainable business in Europe, we also need to continue to simplify our governance, reduce costs and drive efficiencies," he added.

Ford-Werke GmbH currently operates major plants in Cologne and Saarlouis. The subsidiary is involved in the production of vehicles like the Fiesta and Focus, as well as engine manufacturing.

Shares of Ford Motor (F) were down 1.4% in premarket trading on Monday. The auto stock is down about 20% over the last 52 weeks. The dividend yield for new buyers of the stock is 7.58%.

data.title: Beneficient settles GWG litigation
data.description:
Beneficient (NASDAQ:BENF) has entered into a binding agreement to settle all claims in the previously disclosed lawsuits relating to GWG Holdings in federal court in the Northern District of Texas and the Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of Texas.

Beneficient's (NASDAQ:BENF) decision to enter into this settlement agreement was based on eliminating ongoing costs, distractions, and other risks inherent in litigation, the company said.

Following the settlement of the GWG Litigation, other GWG-related claims against parties other than the company, its subsidiaries, and each of their current and former directors and officers, including its founder and CEO, remain outstanding, including certain claims against entities related to Beneficient’s founder and CEO to whom Beneficient owes certain indemnification obligations.

The company intends to support a vigorous defense against such claims, it said.

data.title: Frontera Energy GAAP EPS of -$0.36, revenue of $216.37M
data.description:
• Frontera Energy press release (OTCPK:FECCF): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.36.
  • Revenue of $216.37M (-9.9% Y/Y).
  • Declared Quarterly Dividend of C$0.0625 Per Share, or $3.4 Million in Aggregate, Payable on or around April 16, 2025

data.title: Liverpool enters multi-year kit deal with adidas to replace Nike
data.description:
Soccer club Liverpool has signed a new multi-year partnership with adidas (OTCQX:ADDYY) starting next season, replacing Nike (NKE). 

The new agreement will be worth more than 60 million pounds per year, according to media reports. 

adidas and Liverpool have partnered before, namely from 1985 to 1996 and again from 2006 to 2012.

"Everyone at the club is incredibly excited to welcome adidas back into the LFC family. We have enjoyed fantastic success together in the past and created some of the most iconic LFC kits of all time," CEO of LFC, Billy Hogan, said.

The new partnership will start from August 1. 

"We are very excited that adidas and Liverpool Football Club are teaming up once again. The club is one of the biggest and most iconic names in world football, with a huge fan base. We are honored to once again provide the players with cutting-edge technology to perform at the highest level," adidas CEO Bjørn Gulden said.

data.title: Willis Lease Finance reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Willis Lease Finance press release (NASDAQ:WLFC): Q4 GAAP EPS of $2.81.
  • Revenue of $152.8M (+33.7% Y/Y).
  • Book value per diluted weighted average common share outstanding increased to $80.74 at December 31, 2024, compared to $67.73 at December 31, 2023.

data.title: Veren, Whitecap Resources to merge in all-stock deal valued at ~$15B
data.description:
Veren (NYSE:VRN) and Whitecap Resources (OTCPK:SPGYF) announced a merger agreement Monday in an all-share transaction valued at ~$15B including debt to create a leading light oil and condensate producer in the Alberta Montney and Duvernay plays.

Under the deal terms, Veren (NYSE:VRN) shareholders will receive 1.05 common shares of Whitecap (OTCPK:SPGYF) for each Veren common share held.

The combined company will be led by Whitecap's (OTCPK:SPGYF) existing management team under the Whitecap name with four Veren (VRN) directors to join the Whitecap board, including the current Veren President and CEO Craig Bryksa.

The company will have 370K boe/day of cmbined production, and becomes the largest Canadian light oil focused producer as well as the seventh largest producer in the Western Canadian Sedimentary Basin, with significant natural gas growth potential.

The combined company becomes the largest producer in the Kaybob Duvernay and Alberta Montney with 220K boe/day and the second largest producer in Saskatchewan.

Veren (VRN) and Whitecap (OTCPK:SPGYF) said the anticipate annual synergies of more than $200M can be achieved independent of commodity prices.

data.title: Traton SE reports FY results
data.description:
• Traton SE press release (OTCPK:TRATF): FY EPS rose to €5.61 compared with €4.90 in the previous year
  • Revenue of €47.5B (+1.3% Y/Y).
  • The company proposes to increase the dividend for fiscal year 2024 to €1.70 per share, after paying out €1.50 per share for fiscal year 2023.

data.title: Acer’s February sales rise nearly 6% Y/Y
data.description:
Acer (OTC:ACEYY) has reported consolidated revenues of NT$17.07B for February, up 13.3% month-on-month and 5.9% year-on-year.

Consolidated revenues for year-to-February reached NT$32.15B with 2.6% growth Y/Y, where Chromebook revenues grew 11.9% Y/Y. Total revenues from businesses other than personal computers and displays contributed 32.3% of the group's total revenues in the month and 36.1% year-to-February with 9.5% growth Y/Y.

Source: Press Release
